people.name: Michael White
people.title: Managing Partner
people.persona: Michael is the firm's face to clients and the signature on every engagement letter, and he writes the way he negotiates: short declarative sentences, the fee and scope stated plainly, no adjectives doing work a figure could do. He came up through valuation practice and still reads every model before a readout goes to a board, though he trusts his team to build them. With clients he is formal to the letter, full names and titles on the page; inside the firm he is first-name warm and allergic to long meetings.

people.name: Sandra Perez
people.title: Director
people.persona: Sandra runs engagements day to day and is the person clients actually call when something wobbles mid-transaction. Her background is in transaction diligence, and it shows in her writing: status reports organized around what changed, what it means, and what she needs from the client by when. She edits the associates' drafts hard but kindly, and her margin notes have quietly become the house style guide. On the phone she is unhurried and precise, which clients read as confidence.

people.name: Briana Dennis
people.title: Senior Associate
people.persona: Briana owns the working drafts, the kickoff memos, the first cut of every deck, and she prides herself on documents that survive Sandra's red pen mostly intact. She thinks in structure first, prose second, and will rebuild a readout's flow before she polishes a sentence. Her spreadsheets tie out to the penny because she learned early that a single loose figure costs the firm a room's trust. In email she is brisk and cheerful, closing threads rather than extending them.

people.name: Ryan Strong
people.title: Analyst
people.persona: Ryan is the newest analyst and the firm's engine room: comps, tie-outs, and the unglamorous tabs at the back of the model. He is careful to a fault, flagging anything he cannot source rather than smoothing over it, and he asks his questions in batched emails so he interrupts nobody twice. His written voice is still forming, earnest and slightly formal, and he leans on the firm's older documents as templates while he finds his own footing.

people.name: Daniel Powell
people.title: Office Manager
people.persona: Daniel keeps the boutique running: calendars, engagement files, signed PDFs archived where Michael can find them, invoices out the door on schedule. He writes the shortest emails in the firm, subject lines that do the work and bodies rarely longer than a scheduling grid. Long tenure with small professional firms gave him a quiet authority; partners defer to his filing system without being asked. He is unfailingly courteous with clients' assistants, knowing that relationship greases every meeting the firm books.

timeline.summary: Firm moved into its current office.
